**Title: *The Hollow Shore***

The town of Marrow’s End clung to the edge of the world like a forgotten memory. It was a place where the sea whispered secrets to those who dared to listen, and where the wind carried voices from a time long past. The last light of the day bled into the horizon, painting the sky in hues of bruised purple and bleeding orange. Detective Elias Vane stood at the edge of the cliffs, the salt air biting at his skin like a ghost.

He had been called to the town for the third time in as many months. The disappearances had started with the fisherman, old Tom Hargrove, who vanished without a trace after returning from a storm-lashed night out at sea. Then came the young woman, Lila Marlow, who had left her apartment one morning and never returned. Now, the third—Mara Voss, a schoolteacher—had gone missing, her apartment found locked from the inside, the door slightly ajar as if someone had just stepped out.

The locals had grown quiet, their usual gossip replaced by wary glances and hushed conversations. The sea, once a source of livelihood, now seemed to hold its breath.

They spoke in hushed tones about the cove’s history—of ships lost to the fickle tides, of fishermen who never returned from their last trip, and of the fog that clung to the cliffs like a living thing. He didn’t move as Vane approached. The detective had seen that look before—lost, hollow, as though the world had slipped away from them.
